ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stock_data" (
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the stock data */,
  "symbol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the stock symbol */,
  "open_price" REAL /* Opening price for the day */,
  "high_price" REAL /* Highest price during the day */,
  "low_price" REAL /* Lowest price during the day */,
  "close_price" REAL /* Closing price for the day */,
  "adjusted_close_price" REAL /* Adjusted closing price for the day */,
  "volume" INTEGER /* Volume of stocks traded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("date", "symbol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stock_symbols" (
  "symbol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stock symbol */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* The stock symbol (e.g., AAPL for Apple Inc.) */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company associated with the stock symbol */,
  "exchange" TEXT /* Stock exchange where the stock is traded */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector classification for the stock */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("symbol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., investor, analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* User password for secure authentication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the alert */,
  "symbol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stock symbol the alert is for */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., price, volume) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the alert */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was created */,
  "notification_method" TEXT /* Method for sending notifications */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_symbol_id FOREIGN KEY ("symbol_id") REFERENCES stock_symbols ("symbol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "portfolios" (
  "portfolio_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each portfolio */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who owns the portfolio */,
  "portfolio_name" TEXT /* Name of the portfolio */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the portfolio was created */,
  "risk_level" TEXT /* Risk level associated with the portfolio */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("portfolio_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_portfolios_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "portfolio_stocks" (
  "portfolio_stock_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stock in a portfolio */,
  "portfolio_id" INTEGER /* ID of the portfolio the stock belongs to */,
  "symbol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stock symbol */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date when the stock was purchased */,
  "shares" INTEGER /* Number of shares purchased */,
  "purchase_price" REAL /* Price per share at purchase */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("portfolio_stock_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_portfolio_stocks_symbol_id FOREIGN KEY ("symbol_id") REFERENCES stock_symbols ("symbol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_portfolio_stocks_portfolio_id FOREIGN KEY ("portfolio_id") REFERENCES portfolios ("portfolio_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "symbol_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stock symbol the prediction is for */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date of the prediction */,
  "predicted_price" REAL /* Predicted price for the stock */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the prediction */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_symbol_id FOREIGN KEY ("symbol_id") REFERENCES stock_symbols ("symbol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action performed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the action */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
